answers: Photography basics: learn the rule of thirds, use natural light, keep your background clean, and experiment with angles. Practice daily!
For beginners, start with your phone camera and learn composition first. Then upgrade to a mirrorless camera like Sony A6000 or Canon M50.
Good photos need good light. Shoot during golden hour (sunrise/sunset), avoid harsh midday sun, and always focus on your subject's eyes for portraits.
Edit with free tools like Snapseed (mobile) or Lightroom (desktop). Start with basic adjustments: exposure, contrast, and white balance.
